import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut nums: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut current = 0
  mut has_digit = false
  mut negative = false
  while i <= n
    mut b = 32
    if i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(x)
        b = x
      when None
        b = 32
      end
    end
    if b >= 48 and b <= 57
      current = current * 10 + (b - 48)
      has_digit = true
    else if b == 45 and not has_digit
      negative = true
    else
      if has_digit
        if negative
          nums = list.push(nums, 0 - current)
        else
          nums = list.push(nums, current)
        end
        current = 0
        has_digit = false
        negative = false
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  nums
end

fn insert_sorted(_ sorted: List<Int>, _ x: Int) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut inserted = false
  for y in sorted
    if not inserted and x <= y
      result = list.push(result, x)
      inserted = true
    end
    result = list.push(result, y)
  end
  if not inserted
    result = list.push(result, x)
  end
  result
end

fn sort_list(_ items: List<Int>) -> List<Int>
  mut sorted: List<Int> = []
  for x in items
    sorted = insert_sorted(sorted, x)
  end
  sorted
end

fn format_list(_ items: List<Int>) -> String
  mut out = ""
  mut first = true
  for x in items
    if first
      out = "#{x}"
      first = false
    else
      out = "#{out} #{x}"
    end
  end
  out
end

let line = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let nums = parse_ints(line)
let sorted = sort_list(nums)
println(format_list(sorted))
